title: Attributes should restrict the targets they apply to
summary: An attribute declared with AttributeTargets.All can be placed on assemblies, classes, methods, parameters, return values, and more. Almost no attribute is meaningful in every one of those positions, so the broad target list lets the attribute be applied where it does nothing and hides genuine misuse from the compiler.
List only the program elements the attribute is designed for in its AttributeUsage.
messageFormat: Restrict this attribute to the program elements it actually supports instead of 'AttributeTargets.All'.
